channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Marie-Philip Poulin Leads Montreal Victoire One Win from Walter Cup Title
item.description: Montreal Victoire captain Marie-Philip Poulin has been instrumental in her team's quest for their first Walter Cup. In Game 2 of the finals against the Ottawa Charge, Poulin made a decisive no-look pa...

item.title: Prospective Changes to Constitution Could Shift Key Powers Back to Federal Government
item.description: The latest discussions in Pakistani political circles suggest that a so-called '28th Amendment' or legislative package may be under consideration, even though both PPP and PML-N publicly deny any form...

item.title: Oil Prices Rise Amid Middle East Tensions Following UAE Nuclear Plant Attack
item.description: Oil prices surged to their highest levels in two weeks after a drone attack targeted the Barakah nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates, causing fresh concerns over Middle East stability. Bre...

item.title: Labour Leadership Contenders Spark Criticism Over EU Rejoining Comments
item.description: Fears of a Brexit reversal have intensified following statements from Labour's leading leadership candidates, Andy Burnham and Wes Streeting, suggesting Britain should eventually rejoin the European U...

item.title: WHO Continues to Assess Hantavirus as Low-Risk Threat
item.description: The World Health Organization (WHO) has maintained its assessment that hantavirus currently poses a low risk to global public health. This statement comes amid ongoing monitoring of the virus, which i...

item.title: Meta introduces parental supervision tools for teen social media accounts with limitations
item.description: Meta has rolled out new features aimed at giving parents greater oversight of their children's activity on Facebook, Instagram, Messenger, and Horizon. The updates include enhanced age verification us...

item.title: EACOP Pipeline Project Nears Completion, Creating Jobs and Opportunities in Tanzania
item.description: The East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP) project is now approaching its final stages, according to officials from Tanzania's Ministry of Energy. During an inspection visit to the Chongoleani area i...

item.title: Prominent Tribal Chief and Two Others Killed in Wana Market Explosion
item.description: In a tragic incident in Lower South Waziristan, at least three people were killed and four others injured when an improvised explosive device (IED) detonated in Rustam Bazaar, Wana. Among the deceased...

item.title: Exxaro inaugurates new shaft at Matla Mine as Mantashe affirms coal industry’s ongoing evolution
item.description: Mineral and Petroleum Resources Minister Gwede Mantashe has reaffirmed the relevance of South Africa's coal industry amid the official opening of Exxaro Resources' New Mine 1 at Matla coal mine in Mpu...

item.title: Senate Faces Political Turmoil Amid Bato dela Rosa and Cayetano Controversies
item.description: The Philippine Senate is currently in political turmoil following recent developments involving Senators Bato dela Rosa and Alan Peter Cayetano. Dela Rosa evaded an International Criminal Court arrest...

item.title: 'The Love That Remains': A Poignant Look at a Family in Transition
item.description: Icelandic filmmaker Hlynur Palmason presents 'The Love That Remains' (2025), a quietly powerful exploration of family life amid change. The film centres around Anna, a farmer and artist, and her forme...

item.title: India and Netherlands Strengthen Ties with 17 Strategic Agreements
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi concluded his visit to the Netherlands on Sunday, achieving a total of 17 strategic agreements covering areas such as migration, mobility, green hydrogen, and defence coop...

item.title: Supreme Court blocks P17-billion SMS refund directive against telcos
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The Supreme Court of the Philippines has upheld a Court of Appeals decision that prevents the National Telecommunications Commission (NTC) from enforcing a 2012 order requiring m...

item.title: Linus Torvalds Discusses Challenges of Managing AI-Generated Linux Kernel Bug Reports
item.description: In a recent announcement on the Linux kernel mailing list, Linus Torvalds highlighted a growing issue with AI-generated bug reports. The flood of automated reports has overwhelmed the kernel security ...

item.title: Updates on the Countrywide Transport Strike by Matatu Operators
item.description: Matatu owners and other petroleum product users have called for a nationwide strike starting Monday to protest the high fuel prices affecting transport costs. The strike is expected to disrupt movemen...

item.title: Fire in Sasaram-Patna Passenger Train in Bihar Causes No Casualties
item.description: On Monday, a fire broke out in the Sasaram-Patna passenger train at Sasaram Junction in Bihar due to a suspected short circuit. Fortunately, there have been no casualties reported so far. This inciden...

item.title: Early Life and Career of Jean Harlow in the 1920s
item.description: Jean Harlow, born Harlean Carpenter on March 3, 1911, in Kansas City, Missouri, began her journey to stardom in the late 1920s as a teenage extra and bit player in silent films. Before becoming the ic...

item.title: Nigeria Wins 29 Medals at African Weightlifting Championships
item.description: Nigeria don show say dem still dey strong for African weightlifting as dem finish di 2026 African Senior Weightlifting Championships wey hold for Ismailia, Egypt. Di team wey carry 10 athletes plus th...

item.title: Senator Bato dela Rosa’s Appearance Before Senate Impeachment Court Remains Possible, Lawyer Says
item.description: Senator Ronald “Bato” dela Rosa’s lawyer, Israelito Torreon, stated that it is ultimately up to the senator whether he will appear before the Senate as it convenes as an impeachment court this week. T...

item.title: Factors Behind Yoruba Business Success and Wealth Creation
item.description: Dis article talk about why the Yoruba people dey among the most successful business ethnic groups for Africa. Sunday Adelaja show say e no just be about money, but systems, culture, and positioning. Y...
